title: viola × hungarica
intro: Viola × hungarica is a hybrid species of violet, derived from the cross between Viola hirta and Viola latifolia, native to Eastern Europe. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which are attributed to the presence of flavonoids and saponins. The health benefits of Viola × hungarica include its ability to soothe digestive issues, reduce anxiety and stress, and promote wound healing. The plant can be prepared as a herbal tea, tincture, or salve, and is often used in folk medicine to treat various ailments.
